text: Regulatory and trade policy developments present near-term risks for Apple's supply chain and pricing strategy. President Trump is preparing industry-specific tariffs to kick in alongside country-by-country duties by August 1, 2025, which could impact Apple's manufacturing costs and product pricing. Analysts noted that iPhone demand has been propped up by pull-ahead buying ahead of these anticipated tariffs. Meanwhile, G-20 finance ministers committed to international policy cooperation amid escalating tensions over the US trade war, though consensus remains rare. Despite these headwinds, Zacks continues to highlight Apple as a top pick, citing Services growth as a key driver of investor interest.
